mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers discount rates and supports equity multiples, so further declines in policy rates predict positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive on average when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates weakening household income and demand, which reduces revenue expectations and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the average forward return is not negative in the triggered sample.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS and RSAFS both improve together, payroll gains and stronger retail sales jointly indicate firm nominal demand, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-RSAFS signal does not outperform either series alone, or if the 60-day forward return is not positive when both series are rising.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and capacity utilization, which raises cyclical earnings expectations and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive INDPRO increases are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if rolling Rank IC is below 0.03 for any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: If CPIAUCSL inflation stays elevated while FEDFUNDS remains near the zero bound, real-rate repression supports risk assets and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation is positive and FEDFUNDS < 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if episodes with positive inflation and near-zero FEDFUNDS do not show above-baseline 20-day SPX gains, or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in HOUST signals a housing demand contraction that leads broader cyclical activity, so falling housing starts predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the signal's hit rate is indistinguishable from 50%.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in UNRATE and decline in PAYEMS identifies a labor-market deterioration that weakens earnings and consumer demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises and PAYEMS falls over the same 3-month window
failure_condition: Reject if the joint deterioration signal fails to outperform either labor series alone, or if triggered windows do not yield negative average 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: In an industrial upturn, rising INDPRO together with rising PAYEMS confirms real activity expansion and should lift cyclical earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO-PAYEMS signal does not produce positive average 20-day SPX returns, or if its predictive value vanishes after controlling for the individual series.
